sections.title: Introduction
sections.content:
This Software Requirements Document (SRD) defines the objectives, scope, and high-level requirements for the "spec-designer" project, which is developed using the Go programming language. The purpose of this document is to provide a clear and comprehensive understanding of the system’s intended functionality, guiding both the development team and stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle.

The SRD outlines the system’s goals, target users, and primary use cases, ensuring alignment between business needs and technical implementation. It serves as a foundational reference for subsequent design, development, testing, and maintenance activities. By establishing a shared understanding of requirements, this document aims to minimize ambiguities, reduce project risks, and facilitate effective communication among all parties involved.

This introduction sets the context for the detailed requirements that follow, ensuring that all stakeholders have a common baseline from which to evaluate the system’s success.

sections.title: Overall Description
sections.content:
The Spec Designer is a software system developed in Go, designed to facilitate the creation, management, and validation of technical specifications for software projects. The system provides a structured environment for users to define, edit, and organize specification documents, ensuring consistency, traceability, and collaboration throughout the software development lifecycle.

The primary objectives of Spec Designer are to:

-   Enable users to author and maintain specification documents using an intuitive interface.
    
-   Support version control and change tracking for all specification artifacts.
    
-   Allow for the validation of specifications against predefined rules and templates to ensure compliance and quality.
    
-   Integrate with external tools and repositories to streamline the specification workflow and maintain alignment with project requirements.
    
-   Provide robust access control and user management to safeguard sensitive information and support collaborative editing.
    

Spec Designer is architected as a modular, extensible platform, leveraging Go’s concurrency and performance features to deliver a responsive and scalable solution. The system is intended for use by software architects, engineers, project managers, and other stakeholders involved in the specification and design phases of software projects.

Key features include:

-   Specification document editor with support for rich formatting and structured templates.
    
-   Versioning and audit trails for all changes.
    
-   Validation engine for automated checks and compliance enforcement.
    
-   Integration APIs for connecting with issue trackers, code repositories, and CI/CD pipelines.
    
-   Role-based access control and authentication mechanisms.
    

The system is designed to be deployed in both cloud and on-premises environments, supporting a range of organizational needs from small teams to large enterprises. The architecture emphasizes maintainability, extensibility, and security, ensuring that Spec Designer can evolve alongside the projects it supports.

sections.title: Functional Requirements
sections.content:
### FR-01 — User Registration Form

The system **shall** provide a user registration endpoint (`POST /api/v1/auth/register`) that accepts user-supplied credentials and profile information.

FieldTypeRequiredConstraints`usernamestring`✅3–50 characters, alphanumeric + underscore, unique`emailstring`✅Valid RFC 5322 email format, unique`passwordstring`✅Minimum 8 characters, at least 1 uppercase, 1 digit, 1 special character`full_namestring`✅2–100 characters`phone_numberstring`❌E.164 international format if provided

* * *

### FR-02 — Input Validation

The system **shall** validate all registration input fields server-side before processing.

-   **FR-02.1** — The system shall reject any request where a required field is missing or empty, returning HTTP `400 Bad Request` with a structured error payload indicating which fields failed validation.
    
-   **FR-02.2** — The system shall validate that `email` conforms to a valid email address format.
    
-   **FR-02.3** — The system shall enforce password complexity rules: minimum 8 characters, containing at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character (`!@#$%^&*`).
    
-   **FR-02.4** — The system shall trim leading and trailing whitespace from `username` and `full_name` before validation.
    

* * *

### FR-03 — Uniqueness Check

The system **shall** enforce uniqueness of registration credentials.

-   **FR-03.1** — The system shall verify that the submitted `email` does not already exist in the user store prior to account creation.
    
-   **FR-03.2** — The system shall verify that the submitted `username` does not already exist in the user store prior to account creation.
    
-   **FR-03.3** — If a duplicate is detected, the system shall return HTTP `409 Conflict` with a descriptive error message identifying the conflicting field.
    

* * *

### FR-04 — Password Hashing

-   **FR-04.1** — The system **shall** hash the user's plaintext password using **bcrypt** (minimum cost factor: 12) before persisting it to the data store.
    
-   **FR-04.2** — The system **shall never** store or log the plaintext password at any point during processing.
    

* * *

### FR-05 — Account Creation & Persistence

-   **FR-05.1** — Upon successful validation, the system shall create a new user record in the database containing: a system-generated UUID (`user_id`), `username`, `email`, hashed password, `full_name`, `phone_number` (nullable), `status` (default: `pending_verification`), `created_at`, and `updated_at` timestamps.
    
-   **FR-05.2** — All database operations related to account creation shall be executed within a single atomic transaction to prevent partial writes.
    

* * *

### FR-06 — Email Verification

-   **FR-06.1** — Upon successful account creation, the system **shall** generate a cryptographically secure, time-limited email verification token (validity: 24 hours).
    
-   **FR-06.2** — The system shall send a verification email to the registered address containing a unique verification link in the format: `GET /api/v1/auth/verify-email?token=<token>`.
    
-   **FR-06.3** — The system shall set the account `status` to `active` upon successful email verification.
    
-   **FR-06.4** — The system shall return HTTP `410 Gone` if a verification token has expired or has already been used.
    
-   **FR-06.5** — The system shall provide a resend verification email endpoint (`POST /api/v1/auth/resend-verification`) with a rate limit of **3 requests per hour per email address**.
    

* * *

### FR-07 — Registration Response

-   **FR-07.1** — Upon successful registration, the system shall return HTTP `201 Created` with a response body containing non-sensitive user information: `user_id`, `username`, `email`, `full_name`, `status`, and `created_at`.
    
-   **FR-07.2** — The response **shall not** include the hashed password or any internal system fields.
    

* * *

### FR-08 — Rate Limiting & Abuse Prevention

-   **FR-08.1** — The system shall enforce a rate limit of **10 registration attempts per IP address per hour**.
    
-   **FR-08.2** — Requests exceeding the rate limit shall receive HTTP `429 Too Many Requests` with a `Retry-After` header.
    
-   **FR-08.3** — The system shall log all registration attempts (successful and failed), including IP address, timestamp, and failure reason, for audit purposes.
    

* * *

### FR-09 — Error Handling

All error responses shall follow a consistent JSON structure:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Human-readable description",
    "fields": {
      "email": "Email address is already in use"
    }
  }
}
```

ScenarioHTTP StatusValidation failure`400 Bad Request`Duplicate email / username`409 Conflict`Rate limit exceeded`429 Too Many Requests`Internal server error`500 Internal Server Error`Successful registration`201 Created`

sections.title: 4\. Non-Functional Requirements
sections.content:
### 4.1 Performance

IDRequirementNFR-PERF-01The user registration API endpoint (`POST /api/v1/auth/register`) **must** respond within **500 ms** at the 95th percentile under a concurrent load of **100 requests/second**.NFR-PERF-02Password hashing (e.g., bcrypt) **must** complete within **300 ms** per operation to avoid degrading the overall registration response time.NFR-PERF-03Database write operations for new user records **must** complete within **100 ms** under normal operating conditions.

* * *

### 4.2 Scalability

IDRequirementNFR-SCAL-01The registration service **must** be stateless so that it can be horizontally scaled by deploying multiple Go service instances behind a load balancer without session-sharing concerns.NFR-SCAL-02The system **must** support at least **10,000 new user registrations per day** without architectural changes or manual intervention.

* * *

### 4.3 Security

IDRequirementNFR-SEC-01All passwords **must** be hashed using **bcrypt** with a minimum cost factor of **12** before being persisted to the database. Plain-text passwords **must never** be logged or stored.NFR-SEC-02All registration API traffic **must** be transmitted over **TLS 1.2 or higher**. HTTP connections **must** be rejected or redirected to HTTPS.NFR-SEC-03The registration endpoint **must** enforce rate limiting — no more than **5 registration attempts per IP address per minute** — to mitigate brute-force and enumeration attacks.NFR-SEC-04Email addresses **must** be treated as case-insensitive and normalized (lowercased, trimmed) before storage to prevent duplicate-account exploits.NFR-SEC-05Error responses **must** return generic messages (e.g., `"Registration failed"`) and **must not** reveal whether a specific email address already exists in the system.NFR-SEC-06All input fields **must** be validated and sanitized server-side to prevent SQL injection, XSS, and other injection attacks, regardless of any client-side validation.

* * *

### 4.4 Reliability & Availability

IDRequirementNFR-REL-01The registration service **must** achieve a minimum uptime of **99.9%** (≤ 8.7 hours downtime/year), excluding scheduled maintenance windows.NFR-REL-02Registration failures caused by transient database errors **must** be retried internally (up to **3 attempts** with exponential back-off) before returning an error response to the client.NFR-REL-03Duplicate registration requests (same email, sent concurrently) **must** be handled safely using database-level unique constraints combined with application-level conflict detection, ensuring no duplicate accounts are created.

* * *

### 4.5 Maintainability

IDRequirementNFR-MAINT-01The registration feature **must** be implemented following **Go idiomatic patterns** (interfaces, dependency injection, clean separation of handler / service / repository layers) to facilitate unit testing and future refactoring.NFR-MAINT-02Code test coverage for the registration feature **must** be maintained at a minimum of **80%**, covering both happy-path and error scenarios.NFR-MAINT-03All public functions and types **must** include Go doc-comments (`//`) that clearly describe their purpose, parameters, and return values.

* * *

### 4.6 Observability

IDRequirementNFR-OBS-01The service **must** emit structured logs (JSON format) for every registration attempt, capturing at minimum: timestamp, request ID, outcome (success/failure), and error code. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) such as email addresses **must** be masked or omitted from log output.NFR-OBS-02The service **must** expose a `/health` endpoint returning HTTP `200 OK` when the service and its database connection are healthy, to support load balancer health checks.NFR-OBS-03Key metrics — including registration attempt count, success rate, failure rate, and p95/p99 response latency — **must** be exposed in **Prometheus** format at a `/metrics` endpoint.

* * *

### 4.7 Usability

IDRequirementNFR-USE-01API error responses **must** follow a consistent, machine-readable JSON schema including an `error_code` (string) and `message` (string) field, enabling client applications to display localised, user-friendly error messages.NFR-USE-02Validation error responses **must** identify each invalid field by name so that client UIs can highlight the relevant form fields without additional round trips.

* * *

### 4.8 Compliance & Data Privacy

IDRequirementNFR-COMP-01User personal data collected during registration (e.g., email, full name) **must** be stored and processed in accordance with applicable data-protection regulations (e.g., GDPR, PDPA).NFR-COMP-02The system **must** record the registration timestamp (`created_at`) and the source IP address for audit purposes, retaining this data for a minimum of **90 days**.NFR-COMP-03The system **must** provide a mechanism (API or admin tooling) to permanently delete a user's registration data upon request, in compliance with the _right to erasure_ principle.
